Full document text.

Reviewed by Microsoft's Chief Responsible AI Officer; 6-month update cycle.

Landing page: microsoft.com/en-us/corporate-responsibility/responsible-ai-transparency-report/ (aka.ms/RAITransparencyReport2025).

title: Seemingly Conscious AI Is Coming
model: N/A (position essay by Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman)
notes: MOST digital-minds-relevant item. Full essay text confirmed via fetch (HTTP 200, text/html); opens 'I write, to think...' and closes 'We should build AI for people; not to be a person.' PROVENANCE CAVEAT: self-published on Suleyman's personal domain, not a microsoft.com host, though authored in his capacity as CEO of Microsoft AI and widely treated as Microsoft AI's leadership position on machine consciousness. A republished version exists at project-syndicate.org (2025-09) behind a softer paywall; the personal-site URL is the free, complete canonical text.

title: xAI Risk Management Framework (Draft)
notes: Verified HTTP 200, application/pdf (~231 KB). xAI's first published safety framework, released ~Feb 2025 after the AI Seoul Summit commitment; watermarked DRAFT and never formally implemented. Covers two risk categories: malicious use and LOSS OF CONTROL. Digital-minds-relevant via loss-of-control and deceptive/concerning-propensity discussion (uses benchmarks like MASK honesty, Virology Capabilities Test, WMDP). Superseded by the Aug 2025 RMF and Dec 2025 FAIF; the draft (unlike successors) included eval thresholds.

title: Grok 4 Model Card
notes: Verified HTTP 200, application/pdf (~253 KB). Flagship reasoning-model card. Safety evals grouped as abuse potential, CONCERNING PROPENSITIES (deception, manipulation, sycophancy, political bias/'truth-seeking'), and dual-use (CBRN/cyber). Model-psychology / model-behaviour material rather than any explicit AI-welfare/consciousness/sentience framing.

title: xAI Risk Management Framework
notes: Verified HTTP 200, application/pdf (~144 KB). The finalized RMF that superseded the Feb 2025 draft (dropped the draft's explicit eval thresholds). Structures xAI's approach around abuse potential, concerning propensities (incl. deception), and dual-use capabilities; covers CBRN, cyber, and loss of control; references the MASK honesty benchmark. Loss-of-control section is the main digital-minds-adjacent content.

title: Grok 4 Fast Model Card
notes: Verified HTTP 200, application/pdf (~251 KB). Same three-category safety structure (abuse potential / concerning propensities / dual-use). title: Grok 4.1 Model Card
notes: Verified HTTP 200, application/pdf (~206 KB). MOST digital-minds-relevant xAI card and the most recent full flagship card in the corpus (Grok 4.5 shipped 2026-07-08 but has NO published model-card PDF yet — guessed data.x.ai URLs 404). Text scan confirms dedicated sections on SYCOPHANCY, DECEPTION/dishonesty, MANIPULATION (MakeMeSay), honesty via the MASK benchmark, persuasion, and 'concerning propensities'. Still no explicit AI-welfare/consciousness/sentience discussion — xAI frames these as behavioural/model-psychology risks.

title: xAI Frontier Artificial Intelligence Framework (FAIF)
notes: Verified HTTP 200, application/pdf (~252 KB); confirmed first-page text 'xAI Frontier Artificial Intelligence Framework — Last updated: December 30, 2025' (URL filename says 12-31, doc header says 12-30). Most recent xAI safety framework; written to comply with California's Transparency in Frontier AI Act (TFAIA). Scope = malicious use and LOSS OF CONTROL, incl. catastrophic risk; sets quantitative thresholds/metrics. Loss-of-control content is the digital-minds-adjacent portion.
